preconditions: Verify the Home page is loaded in guest state.
Verify the user is unauthenticated before starting the workflow.

testCases.title: Verify Home guest state opens Login page through the person/profile button navigation flow
steps: Launch the application and navigate to http://localhost/washtabui/home.
Verify the application is in Home guest state and the user is unauthenticated.
Click the person/profile button on the Home page.
Observe the Login page transition and available controls.
testCases.expectedResult: The application transitions from the Home guest state to the Login page through the person/profile button flow and the Username, Password, Login, Back, and Home controls are visible and interactive while the user remains unauthenticated.

testCases.title: Verify Login page controls are visible, enabled, and interactive after opening from Home page
steps: Navigate to the Home page in guest state.
Click the person/profile button to open the Login page.
Click inside the Username field.
Click inside the Password field.
Hover over and click the Login button, Back button, and Home button without submitting credentials.
testCases.expectedResult: The Username field, Password field, Login button, Back button, and Home button are visible, enabled, clickable, and accept user interaction without displaying authentication errors.

testCases.title: Verify password input remains masked during credential entry
steps: Navigate to the Login page from the Home page person/profile button.
Enter krisadmin into the Username field.
Type Carwash#1 into the Password field.
Observe the Password field while entering characters.
testCases.expectedResult: The Password field masks all entered password characters during input entry and does not display the plain text password value.

testCases.title: Verify successful login with approved credentials returns the user to authenticated Home state
steps: Navigate to http://localhost/washtabui/home and confirm guest state.
Click the person/profile button to open the Login page.
Enter Username value krisadmin.
Enter Password value Carwash#1.
Click the Login button.
testCases.expectedResult: The application authenticates the user successfully, returns to the Home page in authenticated state, and authenticated-only UI with authenticated user identity information becomes visible.

testCases.title: Verify authenticated Home page state persists immediately after successful login transition
steps: Open the Login page from the Home page.
Enter valid credentials using krisadmin and Carwash#1.
Click the Login button.
Observe the Home page after the authentication redirect completes.
testCases.expectedResult: The Home page reloads in authenticated state and displays authenticated user identity information and authenticated-only features instead of guest-only unauthenticated state behavior.

testCases.title: Verify invalid username and password combination displays the generic Failed message and blocks authentication
steps: Navigate to the Login page from the Home page person/profile button.
Enter invalid Username value invaliduser.
Enter invalid Password value InvalidPass#1.
Click the Login button.
testCases.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed authentication message is displayed, the user remains unauthenticated, and the application does not transition to authenticated Home state.

testCases.title: Verify blank Username and blank Password submission displays only the generic Failed message
steps: Open the Login page from the Home page.
Leave the Username field empty.
Leave the Password field empty.
Click the Login button.
testCases.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no separate required-field validation message appears, and the user remains unauthenticated on the Login page or in guest state.

testCases.title: Verify submission with only Username populated fails authentication with the generic Failed message
steps: Navigate to the Login page from the Home page.
Enter krisadmin into the Username field.
Leave the Password field empty.
Click the Login button.
testCases.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no separate required-field validation message appears, and the user remains unauthenticated.

testCases.title: Verify submission with only Password populated fails authentication with the generic Failed message
steps: Navigate to the Login page from the Home page.
Leave the Username field empty.
Enter Carwash#1 into the Password field.
Click the Login button.
testCases.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no separate required-field validation message appears, and the user remains unauthenticated.

testCases.title: Verify leading and trailing whitespace handling for Username and Password values
steps: Open the Login page from the Home page.
Enter Username value with leading and trailing spaces around krisadmin.
Enter Password value with leading and trailing spaces around Carwash#1.
Click the Login button.
Observe the resulting authentication behavior.
testCases.expectedResult: The application handles leading and trailing whitespace according to implemented business behavior and the observed result is consistent, stable, and clearly indicates either authenticated Home state or generic Failed authentication behavior without unexpected validation messages.

testCases.title: Verify whitespace-only Username and Password values fail authentication using the generic Failed message
steps: Navigate to the Login page from the Home page.
Enter only whitespace characters into the Username field.
Enter only whitespace characters into the Password field.
Click the Login button.
testCases.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no dedicated required-field validation message appears, and the user remains unauthenticated.

testCases.title: Verify Back button returns the user to the Home page without authentication
steps: Navigate to the Login page from the Home page person/profile button.
Click the Back button on the Login page.
Observe the resulting page state.
testCases.expectedResult: The application returns to the Home page in guest unauthenticated state without displaying authenticated user identity or authenticated-only features.

testCases.title: Verify Home button returns the user to the Home page without authentication
steps: Navigate to the Login page from the Home page person/profile button.
Click the Home button on the Login page.
Observe the resulting page state.
testCases.expectedResult: The application returns to the Home page in guest unauthenticated state without authenticating the user or exposing authenticated-only features.

testCases.title: Verify failed authentication keeps the application on Login flow without authenticated transition
steps: Open the Login page from the Home page.
Enter invalid Username and Password values.
Click the Login button.
Observe the application state after the failure message appears.
testCases.expectedResult: The application does not transition to authenticated Home state, the generic Failed message remains visible, and the user stays unauthenticated on the Login page or equivalent unauthenticated state.

testCases.title: Verify special character input in Username and Password fields does not bypass authentication validation
steps: Open the Login page from the Home page.
Enter special-character-heavy text into the Username field.
Enter special-character-heavy text into the Password field.
Click the Login button.
testCases.expectedResult: Authentication fails safely, the generic Failed message is displayed, the application remains stable, and the user stays unauthenticated.

testCases.title: Verify very long Username and Password values do not authenticate the user and do not break Login page behavior
steps: Navigate to the Login page from the Home page.
Enter a very long Username value exceeding normal credential length.
Enter a very long Password value exceeding normal credential length.
Click the Login button.
testCases.expectedResult: Authentication fails with the generic Failed message or equivalent rejected authentication behavior, the Login page remains responsive, and the user remains unauthenticated.
